body_txt: Hollywood Beauty Standards generally results in actors trying to look their absolute best. But this means attempting to portray an unattractive or even ugly character creates a unique hurdle to overcome. They may use make-up and prosthetics along with unflattering clothes and lighting, but otherwise requires a definite effort to counteract the actor's usual good looks. Since the natural instinct is to "glow up" an actor, it's an interesting challenge for them to portray a Gonk . The rarity of this makes it a form of Dyeing for Your Art , and more cynically Oscar Bait . Actors may waffle between going all in with weight changes and prosthetics, but many are adverse to such a role over a fear of being associated with looking like this . This is not intended to cover deformed or monstrous characters like Freddy Krueger or Quasimodo; that would be The Grotesque . Compare to Irony as She Is Cast . Hollywood Homely is when a character is considered repulsive but the attempt (via plain clothing or hairstyle, glasses , a better-looking sibling, crippling shyness ) is at worst average-looking by non-Hollywood standards. In turn it's often a springboard to reveal the actor's normal appearance for a Beautiful All Along reveal. This trope is when the character is simply not "Hollywood Pretty" and intentionally portrayed as such, with no further comment made. This usually happens in biopics and serious dramas, as there is a greater effort to represent real people and real situations. Compare with Unkempt Beauty , which is more along the lines of "the less makeup you wear, the better you look." If they're playing outright monsters who are usually portrayed as ugly, this may result in a Gorgeous Gorgon . Contrast with Adaptational Attractiveness . Tarnishing Their Own Beauty and Beauty to Beast are the in-universe counterparts.
